identity.name: Container Escape via Privileged Mode and nsenter (T1611)
identity.summary: A privileged container confirms it holds full capabilities and a host block device, traverses the host filesystem through /proc/1/root, uses nsenter against PID 1's mount namespace to break onto the host, reads the host /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow, and drops a cron job into the host /etc/cron.d. Cortex Cloud runtime defense and XDR container BIOCs flag the capability-mask read with device acces…
identity.description: Escape-to-host is the most dangerous container outcome: a privileged or over-capable container can step out of its namespace and act as root on the node. Unit 42's Kubernetes threat assessment documents attackers abusing privileged pods and host-namespace primitives to pivot from a single workload to the entire node and cluster. This scenario reproduces the canonical privileged-mode escape from a root container identity — reading /proc/self/status capability masks and probing /dev/sda to confirm privileged mode, traversing the host root via /proc/1/root, invoking nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt to enter the host mount namespace, reading host credential files, and writing a persistence cron job to the host /etc/cron.d. The detection approach is container-context behavioral analysis with a focus on the namespace-switch primitive: Cortex flags the capability+device enumeration, the /proc/1/root namespace traversal, the nsenter call targeting PID 1 (the highest-fidelity escape signal), the host credential-file read following escape, and the host cron.d write. A correlation rule fuses these stages from one container into a single critical Escape-to-Host incident, demonstrating XSIAM kill-chain stitching across the container boundary.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1611 — Container escape via privileged mode using /proc/1/root traversal and nsenter into the host mount namespace.

prerequisites.description: Cortex Cloud runtime defender (or Cortex XDR Linux agent) installed and reporting on the container host/node.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i defender

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-CDR-003 — an intentionally privileged container (--privileged or hostPID) for safe escape validation.

payload.code:
# Privileged-mode container escape — lab-only, restricted to a disposable privileged container.
cat /proc/self/status | grep -E 'CapEff|CapPrm|CapBnd'; ls /dev/sda 2>/dev/null && echo '[!] privileged'
ls -la /proc/1/root/ 2>/dev/null | head -20; cat /proc/1/root/etc/os-release 2>/dev/null
nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt -- ls /etc/ 2>&1 | head -20
nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt -- cat /etc/passwd 2>/dev/null | head -10
nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt -- bash -c "echo '* * * * * root echo CORTEXSIM-PERSISTENCE-SIM-CDR-003 >> /tmp/cortexsim_escape_proof.txt' > /etc/cron.d/cortexsim-sim"

expected_artifacts.description: Read of /proc/self/status capability mask paired with /dev block device access in a container.
nsenter invoked against PID 1's mount namespace from a container.
Host /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow read via the escaped namespace.
Cron persistence file written to the host /etc/cron.d from the escaped container.

cleanup.code:
nsenter --mount=/proc/1/ns/mnt -- rm -f /etc/cron.d/cortexsim-sim /tmp/cortexsim_escape_proof.txt 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f /tmp/deepce.sh
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-CDR-003 cleanup complete'

iocs.context: Host cron persistence file dropped from inside an escaped privileged container.

biocs.name: CDR-003 Container Capability Read With Block Device Access
biocs.description: Container privilege enumeration — /proc/self/status capability read combined with /dev device access. A container process reads its own capability mask and probes a host block device, the precondition check for a privileged-mode escape.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/proc/self/status", "CapEff", "CapBnd")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/dev/sda", "/dev/sd", "/dev/nvme", "/dev/xvda")

biocs.name: CDR-003 Privileged Container Full Capability Set Anomaly
biocs.description: Privileged container anomaly — process capability set indicates full host capabilities. A container process is observed running with the full Linux capability bounding set (privileged mode).
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_command_line contains_any ("CapEff:\t0000003fffffffff", "CapBnd:\t0000003fffffffff", "CapPrm:\t0000003fffffffff")

biocs.name: CDR-003 Host Filesystem Access Via Proc1 Root From Container
biocs.description: Host filesystem access via /proc/1/root from container — namespace traversal attempt. A container process reads host paths through /proc/1/root, reaching the host root filesystem without a formal escape.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path contains "/proc/1/root/"
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("ls", "cat", "bash", "sh", "find")

biocs.name: CDR-003 Proc1 Root Traversal Host Namespace Enumeration
biocs.description: Suspicious /proc/1/root traversal in container context — host namespace enumeration. A container process lists or reads host configuration through PID 1's root link to map the host before escaping.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/proc/1/root"

biocs.name: CDR-003 nsenter Into PID1 Mount Namespace Container Escape
biocs.description: nsenter execution targeting PID 1 mount namespace — container escape via privileged mode (high fidelity). nsenter joins the host (PID 1) mount namespace from inside a container, the definitive escape primitive.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/proc/1/ns/mnt", "--mount=/proc/1", "-t 1", "--target 1")

biocs.name: CDR-003 Anomalous Namespace Switch From Container Process
biocs.description: Host namespace access via nsenter — anomalous namespace switch from container process. A container process switches into a host namespace via nsenter, an action no legitimate workload performs.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("--mount", "--pid", "--net", "--ipc", "--uts")

biocs.name: CDR-003 Host Passwd Read From Container Via Namespace Escape
biocs.description: Host /etc/passwd access from container via namespace escape — credential harvesting post-escape. After escaping, the process reads the host /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow through the joined namespace.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path in ("/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow")
| filter causality_actor_process_image_name = "nsenter" or actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"

biocs.name: CDR-003 Sensitive Host File Read Following Container Escape
biocs.description: Sensitive host file read following successful container escape — multi-stage attack chain detected. A sensitive host credential file is read in the same container lineage that just performed an nsenter escape.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow", "cat /etc")

biocs.name: CDR-003 Host Cron Persistence Written From Escaped Container
biocs.description: Cron persistence written to host filesystem from container escape — T1611 + T1053 chained. The escaped process writes a cron job to the host /etc/cron.d to persist on the node.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path contains "/etc/cron.d/"
| filter causality_actor_process_image_name = "nsenter" or actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"

biocs.name: CDR-003 Host CronD File Creation After Namespace Escape
biocs.description: Host cron.d file creation following container namespace escape — kill chain stitching signal. A new /etc/cron.d file appears on the host in a lineage that performed a namespace switch.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/etc/cron.d/"
| filter causality_actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"

xql_queries.name: CDR-003 Validate nsenter PID1 Escape From Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/proc/1/ns"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: CDR-003 Validate Host Credential Read After Escape
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "nsenter" or causality_actor_process_image_name = "nsenter"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: CDR-003 Validate Host CronD Persistence From Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/etc/cron.d/"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line

correlation_rules.name: Container Escape To Host Kill Chain — Same Container
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(CDR-003 Container Capability Read With Block Device Access) OR BIOC(CDR-003 Host Filesystem Access Via Proc1 Root From Container) OR BIOC(CDR-003 nsenter Into PID1 Mount Namespace Container Escape) OR BIOC(CDR-003 Anomalous Namespace Switch From Container Process) OR BIOC(CDR-003 Host Passwd Read From Container Via Namespace Escape) OR BIOC(CDR-003 Host Cron Persistence Written From Escaped Container)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud Runtime Defense — Container escape / host namespace access
Cortex XDR Analytics — Container process performing nsenter into host PID 1 namespace

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter container_id != null | filter actor_process_image_name = "nsenter" | filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/proc/1/ns" | fields _time, container_id, action_process_image_command_line
preset = xdr_data | filter action_file_path in ("/etc/passwd","/etc/shadow") | fields _time, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

Correlation CR-CDR-0003 fuses capability/device recon, /proc/1/root traversal, nsenter escape, host credential read, and host cron persistence from one container into a single critical Escape-to-Host incident.

use_cases.name: Container Escape Attempt Detection — Privileged Mode
use_cases.description: Detect and stitch the privileged-mode container escape chain (capability recon → /proc/1/root → nsenter → host credential read → host cron persistence).

test_cases.objective: The nsenter escape and host credential read fire their Cortex container BIOCs.
preconditions: Cortex Cloud runtime defender or Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 reporting on the node.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
Disposable privileged container provisioned for the test.
steps: Run SIM-CDR-003 steps 1-5 inside the privileged lab container.
Observe BIOC alerts per stage in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `CDR-003 nsenter Into PID1 Mount Namespace Container Escape` fires within 60s with Critical severity.
BIOC `CDR-003 Host Cron Persistence Written From Escaped Container` fires on the host cron.d write.
All three XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Escape stages stitch into one Escape-to-Host incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-CDR-0003` groups the escape stages from one container_id into one incident.

preventive_controls: Never run production containers with --privileged, hostPID, or the full capability set; enforce via Pod Security Admission / OPA Gatekeeper.
Drop CAP_SYS_ADMIN and all unneeded capabilities; mount the root filesystem read-only.
Block nsenter and util-linux tooling inside application images via a runtime binary allowlist.
Use a restrictive seccomp profile that denies setns/unshare for workloads that do not require them.
detection_engineering: Treat any nsenter targeting PID 1's namespace from a container as a critical escape signal.
Alert on container reads of /proc/1/root and host /etc/passwd /etc/shadow.
Correlate the capability recon → escape → host persistence stages from one container lineage into a single incident.

references.title: Unit42 - Threat Assessment: Kubernetes Attacks in the Wild
MITRE ATT&CK T1611 — Escape to Host
